m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. Some cats are animals.
2. All animals are mammals.
Conclusions:
I. Some cats are definitely mammals.
II. All cats are mammals.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: From the given statements, as some cats are animals and all animals are mammals, we can conclude that some cats are mammals. Thus, only conclusion I follows.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. All roses are flowers.
2. Some flowers are red.
Conclusions:
I. All roses are red.
II. Some roses are red.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: Although some flowers are red, it does not specify that roses are among these red flowers. Thus, neither conclusion follows logically.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. All spheres are circles.
2. Some circles are squares.
Conclusions:
I. Some spheres are squares.
II. Some squares are circles.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: From statement 2, some circles being squares directly leads to conclusion II. Conclusion I is not supported as there's no information linking spheres directly to squares.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. No pencils are pens.
2. All pencils are erasers.
Conclusions:
I. No erasers are pens.
II. Some erasers are not pens.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: Since all pencils are erasers and no pencils are pens, some erasers (the ones that are pencils) are definitely not pens. Thus, only conclusion II follows.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. Some lions are tigers.
2. All tigers are cats.
Conclusions:
I. Some lions are cats.
II. Some cats are lions.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: From the given statements, some lions being tigers and all tigers being cats imply both conclusions I and II follow logically.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. Some birds are insects.
2. All insects are butterflies.
Conclusions:
I. Some birds are butterflies.
II. All birds are butterflies.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: Since some birds are insects and all insects are butterflies, some birds must also be butterflies. Thus, conclusion I follows.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. All cars are vehicles.
2. Some vehicles are bicycles.
Conclusions:
I. All cars are bicycles.
II. Some cars are bicycles.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: None of the conclusions can be drawn because there is no direct relation given between cars and bicycles.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. Some metals are rods.
2. No rods are brushes.
Conclusions:
I. Some metals are brushes.
II. Some metals are not brushes.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: Since some metals are rods and no rods are brushes, it follows that the metals that are rods are not brushes. Thus, only conclusion II follows.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. All parrots are birds.
2. Some birds are sparrows.
Conclusions:
I. Some parrots are sparrows.
II. Some sparrows are birds.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: Only conclusion II follows as it is directly derived from the second statement: some birds are sparrows. There is no interaction between parrots and sparrows from the given statements, so conclusion I does not follow.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. Some trees are plants.
2. All plants are living beings.
Conclusions:
I. Some living beings are trees.
II. All living beings are plants.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: From the given statements, some trees being plants implies that they are also living beings. Thus, conclusion I follows. Conclusion II does not follow because not all living beings are plants.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. All sharks are fishes.
2. Some fishes are mammals.
Conclusions:
I. All sharks are mammals.
II. Some sharks are mammals.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: From the given statements, neither conclusion follows, as there is no information connecting sharks directly to mammals.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. No humans are immortal.
2. All immortals are deities.
Conclusions:
I. Some deities are not humans.
II. No deities are humans.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: Conclusion II directly follows, as all immortals (which are deities) are not humans. Hence, conclusion I also follows because some deities (in fact, all) are not humans.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. All keys are locks.
2. Some locks are drawers.
Conclusions:
I. All keys are drawers.
II. Some keys are drawers.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: Neither conclusion can be drawn as there is no direct information linking keys to drawers based on the given statements.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. No books are pens.
2. All papers are pens.
Conclusions:
I. Some books are not pens.
II. No books are papers.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: Conclusion I is directly given in the statements since no books are pens. However, conclusion II doesn't follow as we don't have data supporting a direct link or exclusion between books and papers.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. Some drivers are not teachers.
2. All teachers are engineers.
Conclusions:
I. Some engineers are not drivers.
II. Some drivers are engineers.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: There is no information conclusively linking engineers to drivers or excluding engineers from drivers based on the provided statements.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. All clocks are instruments.
2. No instruments are gadgets.
Conclusions:
I. No clocks are gadgets.
II. All gadgets are clocks.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: Since all clocks are instruments and no instruments are gadgets, it directly leads to conclusion I. Conclusion II is contrary to the basic premises.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. All fruits are vegetables.
2. Some vegetables are pulses.
Conclusions:
I. All fruits are pulses.
II. Some fruits are pulses.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: The statements don't provide enough information to link fruits directly to pulses, so neither conclusion follows.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. All dramas are acts.
2. Some acts are events.
Conclusions:
I. Some dramas are events.
II. All dramas are events.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: There is no direct logical link from dramas to events, so neither conclusion follows.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the statements below:
1. Some tablets are computers.
2. All computers are devices.
Conclusions:
I. Some tablets are devices.
II. Some devices are not tablets.
Which of the following is logically correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows

m_Item1.Explanation: From statement 2, all computers (which include some tablets) are devices, thus some tablets are devices. Not all devices are tablets, confirming conclusion II.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Some pens are pencils. All pencils are erasers. Which conclusion follows?
m_Item1.Explanation: From the given premises, we can conclude that since all pencils are erasers and some pens are pencils, therefore, some erasers are pens.

m_Item1.QuestionText: All roses are flowers. No flowers are weeds. Which of the following conclusions can be drawn?
m_Item1.Explanation: If all roses are flowers and no flowers are weeds, then no roses can be weeds.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Some trains are planes. All planes are cars. Which conclusion is correct based on the statements?
m_Item1.Explanation: From the premises, since all planes are cars and some trains are planes, it follows logically that some trains are cars.

m_Item1.QuestionText: All students are scholars. Some scholars are athletes. Which conclusion can be logically drawn?
m_Item1.Explanation: Since some scholars are athletes, it is logical to conclude that some athletes are also scholars.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Some fruits are vegetables. All vegetables are healthy. Which conclusion follows?
m_Item1.Explanation: Some fruits being vegetables and all vegetables being healthy leads to the conclusion that some fruits are healthy.

m_Item1.QuestionText: All managers are employees. Some employees are not engineers. What can be concluded?
m_Item1.Explanation: Since some employees are not engineers and all managers are employees, some managers could also not be engineers.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Some chairs are tables. No tables are desks. Which of the following is true?
m_Item1.Explanation: No tables being desks implies that some chairs being tables do not relate to desks at all, hence no chairs are desks.

m_Item1.QuestionText: All players are participants. All participants are winners. Which conclusion is correct?
m_Item1.Explanation: All players being participants and all participants being winners means that all players are winners.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Statement: All roses are flowers. Some flowers fade quickly.
Conclusion I: Some roses fade quickly.
Conclusion II: All flowers are roses.

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow.
Neither conclusion I nor II follows.

m_Item1.Explanation: From the information given, neither conclusion can be drawn logically. Roses are a subset of flowers, and fading quickly is related only to some flowers, but it is not specified that any of these are roses.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Statement: Some students are intelligent. No intelligent people are lazy.
Conclusion I: No students are lazy.
Conclusion II: Some students are not lazy.

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow.
Neither conclusion I nor II follows.

m_Item1.Explanation: Since some students are intelligent and no intelligent people are lazy, we can only logically conclude that some students are not lazy.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Statement: No cats are dogs. All dogs are pets.
Conclusion I: No cats are pets.
Conclusion II: Some pets are cats.

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow.
Neither conclusion I nor II follows.

m_Item1.Explanation: The statements give no information about the relationship between cats and pets, only about cats and dogs and dogs and pets. Hence, neither conclusion follows.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Statement: All birds have wings. Some birds can fly.
Conclusion I: All winged creatures can fly.
Conclusion II: Some winged creatures can fly.

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow.
Neither conclusion I nor II follows.

m_Item1.Explanation: The statement identifies that some birds can fly and since birds have wings, it supports that some winged creatures can fly, but not all.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Statement: Some fruits are apples. All apples are nutritious.
Conclusion I: Some fruits are nutritious.
Conclusion II: All nutritious things are fruits.

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow.
Neither conclusion I nor II follows.

m_Item1.Explanation: Since all apples are nutritious and some fruits are apples, it necessarily follows that some fruits are nutritious. Conclusion II is not supported by the statements.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Statement: Some chairs are tables. No table is a cupboard.
Conclusion I: No chair is a cupboard.
Conclusion II: Some chairs are not cupboards.

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow.
Neither conclusion I nor II follows.

m_Item1.Explanation: From the statements, at least some chairs, which are tables, are not cupboards. Therefore, some chairs are not cupboards. Conclusion I is too strong given the information.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Statement: All that glitters is not gold. Some metals glitter.
Conclusion I: Some metals are not gold.
Conclusion II: All that glitters is metal.

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow.
Neither conclusion I nor II follows.

m_Item1.Explanation: Since some metals glitter and it is known that not all that glitters is gold, it follows that some metals are not gold. Conclusion II cannot be deduced from the given statements.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Statement: All books are educational. Some books are graphic novels.
Conclusion I: Some graphic novels are educational.
Conclusion II: All educational things are books.

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow.
Neither conclusion I nor II follows.

m_Item1.Explanation: Since all books are educational, and some books are graphic novels, it follows that some graphic novels are educational.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Statement: Many artists are mad. No mad persons are scientists.
Conclusion I: No artists are scientists.
Conclusion II: Some artists are not scientists.

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow.
Neither conclusion I nor II follows.

m_Item1.Explanation: Since many artists are mad and no mad persons are scientists, some artists (who are mad) are not scientists. Conclusion II follows but not I, as not all artists are necessarily mad.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Statement: Some lizards are reptiles. All reptiles lay eggs.
Conclusion I: Some lizards lay eggs.
Conclusion II: All lizards lay eggs.

Options.OptionText: Both conclusions I and II follow.
Neither conclusion I nor II follows.

m_Item1.Explanation: Since some lizards are reptiles, and all reptiles lay eggs, it follows that at least some lizards lay eggs.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the following statements:
1. Some cats are dogs.
2. All dogs are animals.
Conclusions:
I. Some cats are animals.
II. Some animals are dogs.
Which of the following is correct?

Options.OptionText: Both conclusion I and II follow.
Neither conclusion I nor II follow.

m_Item1.Explanation: From statement 1 and 2, we can deduce that some cats are indeed animals, because all dogs (of which some cats are members) are animals. Additionally, since all dogs are animals, some animals are definitely dogs. Therefore, both conclusions I and II follow.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the following statements:
1. All cats are mammals.
2. Some mammals are lions.
3. All lions are fierce.
Conclusions:
I. Some cats are fierce.
II. Some lions are mammals.
Which of the conclusions can be logically inferred?

Options.OptionText: Only conclusion I can be inferred.
Only conclusion II can be inferred.
Both conclusions I and II can be inferred.
Neither conclusion I nor II can be inferred.

m_Item1.Explanation: From the given statements, all cats being mammals and some mammals being lions do not give any direct relation between cats and lions, hence conclusion I does not follow. However, statement 2 suggests that some mammals are lions, which directly supports conclusion II. Therefore, only conclusion II can be logically inferred.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Consider the given statement: 'All cats are pets. Some pets are dogs.' Which of the following conclusions follows from the given statements?
m_Item1.Explanation: From the statement, 'All cats are pets,' it directly follows that some pets are cats because all cats fall into the category of pets. The statement 'Some pets are dogs' does not imply any direct relationship between cats and dogs, hence conclusions A, C, and D do not follow.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Two statements are given below followed by two conclusions. You have to decide which conclusion/s can be logically derived from the statements and mark the appropriate option.
Statement I: Some vegetables are green.
Statement II: No green is red.
Conclusion I: Some green are not vegetables.
Conclusion II: Some vegetables are not red.
m_Item1.Explanation: See the Venn diagrams for both statements. From the diagrams, it is clear that only conclusion II follows.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
2 statements are given below followed by some conclusions. Assuming statements to be true, decide which conclusion/s is/are true.
Statements: 1: Some cities are clean.        2: Some boxes are clean.
Conclusions: I. Some clean are boxes.    II. All cities are boxes.    III. Some cities are boxes.
IV. Some clean are cities.
m_Item1.Explanation: Only I and IV are true.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Statements: Some actors are singers. All the singers are dancers.
conclusions: Some actors are dancers. No singer is actor.
m_Item1.Explanation:
some actors => singers
all singers => dancers
so, singers are actors
Therfore option A is correct.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Statements: All the harmoniums are instruments. All the instruments are flutes
Conclusions: All the flutes are instruments. All the flutes are instruments

Statements: Some mangoes are yellow. Some tixo are mangoes.
Conclusions: Some mangoes are green. Tixo is Yellow.

Statements: Some ants are parrots. All the parrots are apples.
Conclusions: All the apples are parrots. Some ants are apples
m_Item1.Explanation:
all parrots are apples but there was not given all apples are parrots.
so only conclusion 2 is correct.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Statements: Some papers are pens. All the pencils are pens.
Conclusions: Some pens are pencils. Some pens are papers.

Statements: All the windows are doors. No door is a wall.
Conclusions: Some windows are walls. No wall is a door.

Statements: Some dogs are bats. Some bats are cats.
Conclusions: Some dogs are cats. Some cats are dogs.

Statements: No sky is black. Some black are crows.
Conclusions: Some crows are sky. Some crows are not sky.

Statements: All hopes are alive. All alive are human.
Conclusions: Some human are hopes. Some alive are hopes.
m_Item1.Explanation: Draw venn Diagram

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Statements: Some questions are answers. Some answers are writers. All writers are poets.
Conclusions: Some writers are answers. Some poets are questions. All the questions are poets. Some poets are answers.

Options.OptionText: only (1) and(4)
